rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RAFAEL ZEPEDA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #11, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The accounting does not balance as supplemented, in that total credits exceed total charges. The conservator's second supplement filed after the last hearing disclosed that service fees paid to Vanguard and reported as disbursements during this account period were generated by involuntary sales of partial shares of the estate securities, but no gain (as part of charges) or loss (as part of credits) from each sale was accounted for. It should also be noted that the ending carry value of each security on hand (as part of credits) must be calculated by deducting the prorated carry value (instead of the actual sale price reflecting the fair market value) of the security sold during the account period. The judicial officer is to determine whether the unbalanced account should be allowed (but not approved) as presented, and if so, whether the conservator should be allowed to recalculate and adjust the beginning carry value of each security in his next accounting if necessary to balance. 2) The accounting continues to report the conservatee's wages as receipts and bondable income of the estate, despite the default rule under Probate Code section 2601 that the conservatee's wages are NOT a part of the estate "unless otherwise ordered by the court". The judicial officer is to review the conservator's second supplement filed after the last hearing and discuss with counsel whether the conservatee's wages should be excluded from bondable income of the estate in subsequent accountings. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF SOFIA Y. NUNEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition To Deposit Funds In A Blocked Account
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review the file and may discuss with counsel and determine whether to: 1) Discharge the minor's counsel at this time, given that counsel may have ethical duties not to take or propose any action without discussing first with the minor as counsel's "client" to ascertain her wishes (which counsel is unable to do, as the minor and her mother cannot be reached). 2) Allow the minor's counsel to petition for her fees. 3) Informally invite the public guardian again (or formally issue an order to appear at this time) to discuss: i) The public guardian's appointment as temporary guardian, either on the public guardian's petition for voluntary appointment under Probate Code section 2920(a)(2) or on the Court's own motion under section 2920(b) to give 15-day notice to the public guardian to apply for involuntary appointment. ii) Termination of the guardianship estate, on the public guardian's section 3411 petition as temporary guardian, and deposit of the remaining cash with the county treasurer under section 3412(e) to allow immediate discharge of the public guardian, unless the public guardian decides to continue serving as general guardian until the minor attains majority. 4) Issue an order to show cause to the guardian and her counsel and surety: i) Why the guardian should not be suspended in favor of the public guardian as temporary guardian. ii) Why the guardian should not be surcharged for unexplained disbursements. iii) Why the guardian should not be surcharged an additional amount representing the guardian's counsel's fees for multiple court appearances to explain the guardian's failure to file the subject accounting, if the estate is charged (which happens if the Court approves the fees in full on this petition). iv) Why a surcharge against the guardian, if unpaid, should not be considered a breach of condition of the guardian's bond (see section 2620.2(c)(3)). 5) Continue the accounting petition including the guardian's counsel's fee request until the public guardian is appointed as temporary guardian and takes possession of the estate cash, or take under submission to: i) Merely acknowledge (but not approve or even allow or settle) the account without giving res judicata effect otherwise avaiable under section 7250(a). ii) Reserve the surcharge issue for later determination. iii) Deny the request to reduce the guardian's bond until the surcharge issue is resolved. iv) Fix the guardian's counsel's fees and costs. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD KATZOVITZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (4) The FTB has not withdrawn its Request for Special Notice, only its creditor's claim. (5) The I&A was not filed concurrently with the petition and was just filed on 4/7/25, when it should have been filed no later than last October. (6) What happened to the miscellaneous jewelry appraised at $500? (7) Did Loren Katzovitz sign the waiver of accounting and notice electronically? His "signature" (without any indication that it was electronically submitted) appears entirely different from the one on his declination signed on 3/27/24. (8) The verification by the executor is not for this petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/27/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Letters were issued to the executor on 6/7/24, not 6/4/24. Rev. & Tax. Code Sect. 19513 was repealed in 2018.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT D. DOCKENDORFF 2005 REVOCABLE TRUST, AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Invalidating Trust Restatement, Removal And Replacement Of Trustee, Financial Elder Abuse, Return Of Trust Assetts, Imposition Of Constructive Trust, Prohibiting Trustee From Using Trust Funds, Damags, Surcharge, And Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proper form of notice for a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] The Court is aware that objections were just recently filed. No courtesy copies of either the petition or the objections have been received yet, so they have not been fully reviewed. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) The petition does not appear to propose who the successor trustee should be. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and objections and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/27/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IMOGENE B. GIELING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The original will and codicils do not appear to have been filed yet. (4) Item 3f(2) of the petition appears to be incomplete. (5) Clifford Hansen is missing from item 8 of the petition. He must receive (or waive) notice. [Probate Code Section 8110(b).] (6) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (7) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? (8) An amendment was just filed on 4/9/25. An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. (9) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/27/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHO YEE CHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 05/19/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORRAINE LEE WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Granted without hearing. Status hearing is set for 5/19/2026. Personal Representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration.
